user:
Prove the following Lean 4 theorem. One or more hint lemmas are provided — they are available as admitted theorems in scope. Use any that are helpful.

Imports in scope: LeanSrc.Examples.

### Input

* `<local_ctx>`: Local definitions from the same file that precede the target theorem. You may freely reference any of them without redefining them.

<local_ctx>
def drop (n: Nat) (xs: List α) : List α :=
  match n, xs with
  | 0, _ => xs
  | _, [] => []
  | n + 1, _ :: xs => drop n xs

def length (xs: List α) : Nat :=
  match xs with
  | [] => 0
  | _ :: xs => 1 + length xs
</local_ctx>

Theorem statement:

    (n: Nat) (xs: List α) (ys: List α) : (drop n (xs ++ ys) = drop n xs ++ drop (n - length xs) ys)

### Hint Lemmas

response:
<reasoning>
Prove by induction on xs. Base case xs = [] is given by hint lemma_hint_14. Step case xs = x :: xs uses hint lemma_hint_2, which lifts the induction hypothesis to the cons case for all n.
</reasoning>

<lean4_proof>
  intro n xs ys
  induction xs with
  | nil =>
      simpa using (lemma_hint_14 (α := α) n ys)
  | cons x xs ih =>
      simpa using (lemma_hint_2 (α := α) ys x xs ih n)
</lean4_proof>
